text: As artificial intelligence becomes more common in our daily lives, important ethical questions arise. One major issue is bias. If an AI system is trained on data that contains unfair patterns, it can make decisions that discriminate against certain groups. This is why fairness must be built into the development process. Another concern is privacy. AI often collects personal data, so people need to know how their information is used and protected. Transparency is also crucial—users should understand how an AI reaches its conclusions. Responsible AI development means that companies and engineers must consider all these factors carefully. By addressing bias, ensuring fairness, protecting privacy, and being transparent, we can create AI systems that are both powerful and ethical.
